free_text: Neutron resonance spectroscopy of 103Rh from 30 eV
to 2 keV
Data were measured at flight path 2
of the Manuel Lujan Neutron Scattering Center at Los
Alamos.
Target was assembled from plates of natural rhodium
metal (100% 103Rh, 99.9% chemically pure) in the
form of a 10-cm diameter disk of areal density 2.99
g/cm2.
Detector is an array of 24 CsI(pure)
crystals placed around the interaction target,
providing approximately 3.0-77 of detection solid
angle. The crystals are packed tightly around the
target in two arrays of 12 crystals each.
The neutron energy was determined from the
time-of-flight over a 59.4-m distance from source to
detector.
The extracted proton beam strikes a tungsten
target and neutrons are created by the spallation
process. Neutrons are then moderated using a chilled
water moderator and collimated. Stacked proton pulse
extracted from Proton Storage Ring was reduced
in width to about 250 ns.
Time of flight spectra were analyzed using code FITXS
developed by TRIPLE collaboration to determine
resonance parameters from transmission or capture
data. All major resolution effects and effects of
moderation time, detector efficiences, beam length
target temperature are included in FITXS.
No information given
Compiled by S.H.
Data taken from tab. 1 of the reference
